description: Local LLM tier definitions for hardware-aware orchestration via llama-server (llama.cpp). Models picked for a <N> GB VRAM ceiling (<GPU model>). Edit the runtime/tier values to match your hardware.

runtime.note: No per-request model swap: serialize model loads under the VRAM ceiling. An external launcher owns the process lifecycle; switching tiers means stopping one llama-server and starting another. Default port 8080 (set with --port).

reasoning.purpose: Multi-step Chain of Thought, strategic planning, adversarial code review, complex problem decomposition.
use_when: task is an adversarial review (find what could be wrong)

tooling.purpose: High-accuracy tool calling, JSON formatting, code implementation, surgical multi-file search. Grammar-constrained JSON via response_format json_schema.
use_when: task is implementation from a clear spec
task is high-volume code-fragment search across files

general.purpose: General chat, summarization, classification — when neither reasoning nor tooling specialization is needed.
general.note: gemma-4-E4B is the strongest small model on structured tasks here; the older gemma3-4b-Q4_K_M.gguf may fail to load on some llama-server builds (missing hyperparameter key) — prefer gemma-4-E4B.

stretch_warning: Models >=14B partial-offload on a <N> GB VRAM ceiling. Inference drops to <5 t/s and risks OOM under VRAM contention (gaming, browser GPU). Avoid unless you've confirmed nothing else is using the GPU.
